metadata.title: JRN FK-Shuffling Confound Control on rel-f1
metadata.description: Tests whether JRN captures genuine relational join structure or merely reflects child-table feature quality. For each of 13 FK joins x 3 driver tasks in the rel-f1 dataset, compute normal JRN via LightGBM probes, then compute shuffled JRN (FK column permuted to break true parent-child associations). Decompose JRN into structural component (destroyed by shuffling) and feature component (survives shuffling). Statistical tests confirm whether the structural component dominates.

per_join_results.interpretation: MODERATE structural component (structural=0.0125, feature=0.2355, normal_jrn=1.2480). Circuit characteristics affect race outcomes; indirect driver connection via bridge table.
LOW structural component (structural=0.0001, feature=-0.0226, normal_jrn=0.9774). Constructor standings by race; indirect connection to drivers.
LOW structural component (structural=0.0000, feature=-0.0183, normal_jrn=0.9817). Constructor standings for constructor; indirect driver connection.
HIGH structural component (structural=0.0531, feature=0.1923, normal_jrn=1.2454). Race context for standings provides temporal signal about championship progression.
HIGH structural component (structural=0.1637, feature=0.1719, normal_jrn=1.3356). Championship standings are driver-specific accumulations; strong structural signal expected.
LOW structural component (structural=-0.0000, feature=-0.0205, normal_jrn=0.9794). Constructor race results; indirect driver connection.
LOW structural component (structural=0.0000, feature=-0.0145, normal_jrn=0.9855). Constructor-level results; indirect driver connection.
MODERATE structural component (structural=0.0435, feature=0.1830, normal_jrn=1.2265). Race context for qualifying provides circuit-specific qualifying patterns.
HIGH structural component (structural=0.1532, feature=0.2032, normal_jrn=1.3564). Qualifying positions directly reflect individual driver pace.
LOW structural component (structural=0.0000, feature=0.0000, normal_jrn=1.0000). Constructor affects qualifying through car performance; moderate structural signal.
MODERATE structural component (structural=0.0429, feature=0.1973, normal_jrn=1.2402). Race features (circuit, weather) affect all drivers equally; moderate structural signal.
HIGH structural component (structural=0.1306, feature=0.2352, normal_jrn=1.3658). Race results are highly driver-specific; position/points directly tied to individual driver performance.
LOW structural component (structural=0.0000, feature=0.0000, normal_jrn=1.0000). Constructor affects results through car competitiveness; structural but shared across team drivers.

results.conclusion: Structural component is STATISTICALLY SIGNIFICANT but feature component is larger in magnitude (paired t-test p=0.000109, Cohen's d=0.69, structural dominant in only 5% of pairs). Normal JRN is reliably higher than shuffled JRN (mean structural=0.0461), confirming genuine structural signal exists. However, the feature component (mean=0.1033) — improvement from child-table features regardless of FK correctness — is larger for most joins. JRN captures BOTH structural and feature components, with the structural component being a significant but secondary contributor.
